title: malus coronaria subsp. ioensis
intro: Malus coronaria subsp. ioensis, also known as the Wild Crabapple, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the Rosaceae family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help to soothe digestive issues and promote wound healing. The health benefits of Malus coronaria subsp. ioensis include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and provide antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, including colds, flu, and skin irritations.
